objective: Determine, with the smallest possible analytic and numerical workload, whether free dispersive idle evolution in a qubit-storage model can produce a pi conditional phase difference between the n=0 and n=1 cavity manifolds over a finite time, and document that result as a repository-internal model-validation note.
hypotheses: If chi is nonzero, free dispersive evolution alone produces a pi conditional phase difference at t_pi = pi / |chi|.
A tiny cqed_sim confirmation based on the dispersive_phase helper and a minimal dispersive model will match the analytic phase within numerical precision.
An independent static-Hamiltonian evolution path within the same model will reproduce the same phase trace to numerical precision.
Using chi = -2*pi*2.84 MHz will place the first pi crossing near 176 ns, keeping the probe comfortably under the two-minute compute budget.
assumptions: Closed-system idle evolution with no applied drive or decoherence.
First-order dispersive Hamiltonian is sufficient for the n=0 versus n=1 phase-difference question.
Internal calculations use consistent rad/s and seconds units.
Minimal Hilbert-space truncation is adequate because only the lowest two cavity manifolds are probed.
The study does not instantiate a separate coupling-detuning pair and therefore does not claim a hardware-specific dispersive-regime bound.

success_criteria.analytic: Derive the closed-form finite-time condition t_pi = pi / |chi| for nonzero chi.
success_criteria.numerical: Match the analytic relative phase with both a cqed_sim helper path and an independent static-Hamiltonian evolution path to within wrapped-phase numerical precision.
success_criteria.outputs: Generate a compact figure-artifact chain for the helper path and one additional figure-artifact pair for the Hamiltonian cross-check.
success_criteria.runtime: Keep the implementation phase well below two minutes on CPU.

completed_tasks: Initialized the probe study and run directories.
Populated the README and IMPROVEMENTS log with mandatory plan-phase structure.
Audited the local cqed_sim API coverage relevant to the free-dispersive probe.
Produced the first SCIENCE_DIRECTIVE.md for the minimal ANA study.

Prepared the probe data, artifact, and figure output paths for the implementation run.
Executed the lightweight free-dispersive probe script and generated the planned outputs.
Recorded the implementation-phase execution summary and task-run updates.
Validated the saved probe outputs against the analytic phase model and confirmed the pi crossing at 176.05633802816902 ns.
Confirmed stability of the wrapped phase trace under the minimal cavity-truncation variation from n_cav=2 to n_cav=3.
Recorded that an external literature comparison is not required for this internal workflow-validation probe.
Extended the preserved report skeleton, compiled the minimal report PDF, and created the report backup.
Created and smoke-tested the lightweight reproducibility notebook.
Prepared the Science Director review handoff files.
Added an independent static-Hamiltonian evolution cross-check and regenerated the probe outputs for revision iteration 2.
Reframed the study metadata as a model-level validation note and queued the revised evidence for the next validation phase.

Rewrote the report into a complete iteration-2 model-validation note, compiled the refreshed PDF, and updated the review handoff files.
pending_tasks: Respond to the Science Director NEEDS_REWORK directive by broadening the study beyond the current first-order model-validation note.

key_results.validation_sanity: Passed: the saved probe data give delta_phi(0)=0, the first pi crossing at 176.05633802816902 ns, and machine-precision wrapped agreement between the analytic phase law, the helper path, and the independent static-Hamiltonian cross-check.
key_results.validation_convergence: Passed: increasing n_cav from 2 to 3 changed the wrapped phase trace by 0.0 rad over all 51 samples, and the helper and Hamiltonian traces at n_cav=3 differ by 0.0 rad after phase wrapping; no time-step sweep applies because both numerical paths use direct unitary evaluation.
key_results.validation_literature: Not applicable for this internal ANA workflow probe.

compute_notes.plan_budget: The next implementation phase should finish in under 10 seconds wall-clock on CPU and stay far below the two-minute cap.
compute_notes.api_reference: The configured cqed_sim API path under C:/Users/dazzl was stale on this machine; the accessible local copy was found under the current user's Box path.
compute_notes.implementation_scope: This invocation completed the implementation script and generated the planned data, artifact, and figure outputs without advancing to report handoff.
compute_notes.validation_scope: Validation reused the saved CSV and JSON artifact, confirmed machine-precision analytic agreement, and confirmed zero wrapped change under the minimal cavity-truncation variation without any additional heavy numerics.
compute_notes.report_scope: The revision report phase replaced the placeholder scaffold with a complete five-page model-validation note, preserved the prior report in report.tex.bak, and compiled the refreshed PDF after simplifying the appendix artifact table.
compute_notes.notebook_scope: The reproducibility notebook reloads the saved outputs by default and its fast cells were executed successfully during the handoff.
compute_notes.revision_implementation_scope: Revision iteration 2 added a static-Hamiltonian evolution cross-check in the rotating frame, regenerated the cleaned helper figure, and produced one additional figure-artifact pair while stopping before the validation and report rewrite phases.
compute_notes.revision_validation_scope: Revision validation re-checked the saved helper and Hamiltonian artifacts without rerunning the study script, confirmed the exact zero-phase initial condition, the pi sample at 176.056338028169 ns, an analytic-versus-Hamiltonian wrapped mismatch of 8.882e-16 rad, and a helper-versus-Hamiltonian wrapped difference of 0.0 rad, then advanced the watcher-facing status to REPORTING.
